itemdesc-aptitude-glyphstone: Attunes a character to one additional class path.
itemdesc-blackguard-glyphstone: Attunes a character to the Blackguard path when class-bound glyphstones are enabled.
itemdesc-clockmaker-glyphstone: Attunes a character to the Clockmaker path when class-bound glyphstones are enabled.
itemdesc-commoner-glyphstone: Attunes a character to the Commoner path when class-bound glyphstones are enabled.
itemdesc-hunter-glyphstone: Attunes a character to the Hunter path when class-bound glyphstones are enabled.
itemdesc-malefactor-glyphstone: Attunes a character to the Malefactor path when class-bound glyphstones are enabled.
itemdesc-retraining-glyphstone: Lets one learned extra class be released after confirmation.
itemdesc-tailor-glyphstone: Attunes a character to the Tailor path when class-bound glyphstones are enabled.
multiclassreborn:gamemechanicinfo-multic:
Multiclass Reborn
A practiced survivor can learn more than one path.

Multiclass Reborn lets your character learn extra classes in addition to their main class. Extra classes can add traits, recipe access, and stat changes, allowing mixed builds such as a hunter who studies metalwork or a tailor who learns combat training.

Opening Class Selection
Press multiclassgui to open class selection. Your main class is listed first, followed by learned extra classes and other available classes. Select a class and choose Learn to add it when you have an open class slot. Some classes may require a class-bound glyphstone; those classes remain visible for comparison, but their Learn button is disabled until a valid glyphstone is used.

Class Slots
Each extra class uses one class slot. Some worlds grant every player the configured maximum number of slots automatically. Other worlds require an Aptitude Glyphstone; use one to unlock one additional extra class slot. Server settings control the maximum number of extra classes.

Class-Bound Glyphstones
Class-bound glyphstones are special glyphstones that point to one or more configured classes. Right-click one to open class selection with its allowed classes available; other classes remain visible, but only an allowed class can be confirmed. The glyphstone is consumed only after the server confirms the class is enabled, not already learned, and allowed by current server rules. If the server allows no extra classes, a class-bound glyphstone may replace the base class when base-class replacement is enabled. Some class-bound glyphstones can also reserve their target classes so generic Aptitude Glyphstones cannot learn them.

Retraining
Extra classes are forgotten from the class selection screen and must be confirmed before anything is removed. On worlds that require glyphstones, confirming the removal consumes one Retraining Glyphstone from your hotbar. If glyphstones are not required, confirmed extra-class removal is free.

Some worlds allow the main class to be forgotten, returning the character to Commoner. Some worlds also allow Commoners to choose a new main class from class selection. Both options are server settings and may be disabled.

Glyphstone Sources
Aptitude Glyphstones and Retraining Glyphstones may be sold by luxury traders. Some servers also enable craftable glyphstone recipes. When enabled, both recipes use loose stones in the corners, a temporal gear above a rough diamond, and gold or silver bits on each side. Aptitude Glyphstones use clear quartz below the diamond; Retraining Glyphstones use a rusty gear below it. Servers can disable generic Aptitude Glyphstones and use class-bound glyphstones from recipes, loot, traders, or admin-granted items while keeping Retraining Glyphstones enabled for paid class forgetting.

Server Rules
Server settings can enable or disable extra-class stat bonuses, recipe traits, glyphstone crafting, generic Aptitude glyphstones, class-bound glyphstones, glyphstone requirements, Commoner base-class choice, and main-class forgetting. Extra-class stat strength can be scaled, and overlapping trait stats can be limited to only the strongest positive bonus or harshest negative penalty.

Commands
Most play can be handled through class selection, but /multiclass summary, /multiclass list, and /multiclass available can be used to check your current slots and class options.
handbook-aptitude-glyphstone-text:
Use an Aptitude Glyphstone to unlock one extra class slot on worlds that require glyphstones. See the Multiclass Reborn guide for details.

When glyphstone crafting is enabled, craft it with a rough diamond in the center, a temporal gear above it, gold or silver bits on each side, clear quartz below it, and four loose stones in the corners. The loose stones may be mixed. Luxury traders may also sell it.
handbook-class-bound-glyphstone-text:
Use a class-bound glyphstone to learn one of its configured classes when class-bound glyphstones are enabled on the server. The picker still shows other classes for comparison, but only a class allowed by the glyphstone can be confirmed. The glyphstone is consumed only after the server confirms the class is enabled, not already learned, and allowed by current server rules.

If the server allows no extra classes, the glyphstone may replace your base class when base-class replacement is allowed. Some servers may require class-bound glyphstones for specific classes or disable generic Aptitude Glyphstones entirely.
handbook-retraining-glyphstone-text:
Use a Retraining Glyphstone to open class retraining. On worlds that require glyphstones, one Retraining Glyphstone is consumed after you confirm a class removal. See the Multiclass Reborn guide for details.

When glyphstone crafting is enabled, craft it with a rough diamond in the center, a temporal gear above it, gold or silver bits on each side, a rusty gear below it, and four loose stones in the corners. The loose stones may be mixed. Luxury traders may also sell it.
multiclassreborn:handbook-aptitude-glyph:
Use an Aptitude Glyphstone to unlock one extra class slot on worlds that require glyphstones. See the Multiclass Reborn guide for details.

When glyphstone crafting is enabled, craft it with a rough diamond in the center, a temporal gear above it, gold or silver bits on each side, clear quartz below it, and four loose stones in the corners. The loose stones may be mixed. Luxury traders may also sell it.

Use a class-bound glyphstone to learn one of its configured classes when class-bound glyphstones are enabled on the server. The picker still shows other classes for comparison, but only a class allowed by the glyphstone can be confirmed. The glyphstone is consumed only after the server confirms the class is enabled, not already learned, and allowed by current server rules.

If the server allows no extra classes, the glyphstone may replace your base class when base-class replacement is allowed. Some servers may require class-bound glyphstones for specific classes or disable generic Aptitude Glyphstones entirely.

Use a Retraining Glyphstone to open class retraining. On worlds that require glyphstones, one Retraining Glyphstone is consumed after you confirm a class removal. See the Multiclass Reborn guide for details.

When glyphstone crafting is enabled, craft it with a rough diamond in the center, a temporal gear above it, gold or silver bits on each side, a rusty gear below it, and four loose stones in the corners. The loose stones may be mixed. Luxury traders may also sell it.
